channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Tuesday convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The first lawsuit is filed over the deadly nightclub roof collapse in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The first known lawsuit has been filed against the owners of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed and killed 231 people in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab And Yehor Konovalov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US blasts UN for extending human rights envoy to Palestinian territories
item.description: The United States is rebuking a United Nations decision to extend Francesca Albanese’s tenure as the U.N. special rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian territories.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: The speed and scope of the federal government’s efforts to terminate the legal status of international students have stunned colleges and universities across the country.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Makiya Seminera And Christopher L. Keller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia sentences 4 journalists tied to Putin rival Navalny to nearly 6 years in prison
item.description: Four journalists were convicted of extremism on Tuesday by a Russian court and sentenced to 5 and a half years in prison for their ties to the country’s deceased opposition leader Alexei Navalny.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump envoy backtracks on remarks about limiting Iran's nuclear program
item.description: Steve Witkoff, President Trump’s point person on talks with Iran, clarified earlier remarks on Tuesday saying the White House could tolerate Iran having a civilian nuclear program, saying it wants Tehran to eliminate its nuclear enrichment and weapons program.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: France expels 12 Algerian officials in tit-for-tat move amid diplomatic tensions
item.description: France says it is expelling 12 Algerian diplomatic officials, a day after Algeria announced the expulsion of the same number of French officials in escalating tensions between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike outside a Gaza hospital kills a medic and wounds several
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of the Kuwaiti Field Hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people — all patients and medics — according to a spokesman for the hospital in the Muwasi area, where hundreds of thousands
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Top Hamas terror leader killed in 'precise strike' by Israel: IDF
item.description: Top Hamas leader Muhammad al-‘Ajlah has been killed in an Israeli strike, the IDF has announced. Al-'Ajilah was responsible for arming terrorists with weapons used to carry out attacks against Israeli civilians and IDF troops, according to the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Witkoff says US 'finally' got answer on Putin's demands for peace
item.description: U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff said Monday that he “finally” got an answer to Russian President Vladimir Putin’s demands for peace during their meeting last week in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Tens of thousands of Jews gather for traditional blessing in Jerusalem
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Tens of thousands of people gathered at the holiest Jewish prayer site in Jerusalem on Tuesday for the traditional priestly blessing prayer. The “Birkat Cohanim,” or Cohen’s blessing, is a ritual dating back over 2,500 years to when King Solomon’s Temple stood on the same site.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many Canadians consider US ‘unfriendly’ amid tariff war: Survey
item.description: Many Canadians consider their southern neighbor “unfriendly” amid tensions over President Trump's trade policies, according to a new survey. In the YouGov survey, 44 percent of respondents said the U.S. is “unfriendly” when asked if they see the country as “an ally or an enemy of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: South Africa's new US envoy called Trump racist, homophobic and narcissistic in a 2020 speech
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South Africa's new special envoy to the United States is already under scrutiny for calling U.S. President Donald Trump a racist, homophobic and narcissistic "right-winger" in a speech in 2020.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singapore dissolves parliament, paving the way for May 3 general election
item.description: Singapore’s parliament was dissolved Tuesday, paving the way for general elections in which the city-state’s long-ruling People’s Action Party will seek to strengthen its dominance under Prime Minister Lawrence Wong.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia claims its deadly attack on Ukraine's Sumy targeted military forces as condemnation grows
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Russia on Monday claimed its deadly missile attack on Ukraine's Sumy that killed and wounded scores including children had targeted a gathering of Ukrainian troops, while European leaders condemned the attack as a war crime.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New York City's mayor visits the Dominican Republic after the deadly nightclub roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams arrived in the Dominican Republic on Monday to pay his respects to the scores of people who died last week when the roof of a popular nightclub collapsed in the country's capital, Santo Domingo.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attack by gunmen leaves at least 40 people dead in Nigeria, the country's president says
item.description: Nigeria’s president says that at least 40 people were killed when Muslim gunmen, believed to be herders, attacked a Christian farming community in the north-central part of the country, the latest in a spate of increasing violence in the West African country.
item.dc:creator: Dyepkazah Shibayan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New report raises questions over the origins of COVID
item.description: A military report revealed that seven U.S. service members had COVID-like symptoms during an event in Wuhan in October of 2019.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: China's Xi Jinping says there are no winners in a tariff war as he visits Southeast Asia
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — China's leader Xi Jinping said no one wins in a trade war as he kicked off a diplomatic tour of Southeast Asia on Monday, presenting China as a force for stability in contrast with U.S.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland says Moscow is 'mocking' Trump with deadly Ukraine strike
item.description: Poland is urging the U.S. to respond to Russian President Vladimir Putin's "mocking" response to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure peace deal by striking civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daniel Noboa is reelected Ecuador's president by voters weary of crime
item.description: QUITO, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuadorian voters weary of crime reelected President Daniel Noboa, a conservative young millionaire with a divisive no-holds-barred crimefighting record, by a wide margin Sunday, but his opponent vowed to seek a recount over what she described as “grotesque” electoral fraud.
item.dc:creator: GONZALO SOLANO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daughter of German-American dissident whose body was mutilated by Iran calls on Trump to nix nuke talks
item.description: The daughter of a California man, Jamshid "Jimmy" Sharmahd, who was executed and mutilated by Iran, wants the U.S. to break off nuclear talks with the clerical regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 30 Ukrainians killed by Russian ballistics in Palm Sunday strike
item.description: Russia hit the Ukrainian city of Sumy with two missiles as civilian celebrated Palm Sunday, killing more than 30 people and injuring dozens of others, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin makes new move to bring South African refugees to US as president blasts nation's rulers again
item.description: As the U.S. begins refugee processing for South African Afrikaners, a group is pushing Washington to support its bid for an autonomous Afrikaner homeland within South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China ramps up military ‘rehearsals’ around Taiwan, outstrips US in air, maritime, space
item.description: Top Indo-Pacific commander warned senators that China's aggression around Taiwan is not an "exercise" but a "rehearsal" for when it finally decides to "reunify" the island with the mainland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tariffs on China are 145%, White House clarifies, as stocks slump
item.description: World markets skyrocketed Thursday in the wake of President Donald Trump's 90-day retaliatory tariff pause.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Wednesday at the Vatican during the royal couple's four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, DANICA KIRKA and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, WGN 9
